pairs.question: Who was Alexander W. Farlinger and what were his primary occupations?
pairs.answer: Alexander W. Farlinger (1861–1925) was a Canadian-born businessman, grocery store owner, and politician in Atlanta, Georgia. He worked as the president of the National Retail Grocers Association, served as a city councilman from 1912 to 1915, and was actively involved in local politics, real estate, and religious organizations.

pairs.question: What notable building did Alexander Farlinger construct in Atlanta, and what became of it?
pairs.answer: Farlinger built the four-story Eclectic Victorian Farlinger Apartments in 1898, designed by George W. Laine. The building initially housed his grocery store on the ground floor and a café on the fourth floor. It was later renamed Hotel Francis, added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1981, and demolished in 1988.

pairs.question: How did the Farlinger Apartments change ownership and purpose over time?
pairs.answer: After Farlinger sold the building in 1910 for $125,000 to William H. McKenzie, president of Atlanta Fertilizer Works, it was renamed 'The Franceis.' By 1930, it had been converted into a hotel.

pairs.question: What were Alexander Farlinger’s contributions to Atlanta’s infrastructure and education?
pairs.answer: Farlinger advocated for the construction of the Spring Street Viaduct and supported Oglethorpe University’s relocation to Atlanta. He served on the university’s Board of Founders.

pairs.question: Describe Alexander Farlinger’s involvement in religious and theological institutions.
pairs.answer: Farlinger served as Secretary for the Atlanta Theological Seminary’s Board of Trustees and was an active member of the Congregational Church. His religious affiliation influenced his community leadership.

pairs.question: What political stance did Alexander Farlinger take regarding racial segregation in housing?
pairs.answer: As a city councilman, Farlinger, along with Claude Ashley, led a 1915 Ku Klux Klan-inspired rally at Grace Methodist Church advocating for racial segregation in housing.

pairs.question: Why did Alexander Farlinger advocate for pure food laws, and what role did this play in his career?
pairs.answer: Farlinger’s advocacy for pure food laws aligned with his responsibilities as a grocery store owner and leader in the National Retail Grocers Association. This stance reflected his commitment to consumer protection and industry standards.
